claim: Behavior rule: In similar future work, apply this decision: Completed read-only audit of MCP runtime freshness/version parity. Package, package-lock, src/dist OS contract, and live MCP responses all report 2.2.1, and installed Codex config points to the local dist/index.js with portable Node v24.18.0 and data root <machine-local-path> Concrete blockers found: many long-running DinoBrain node MCP processes remain alive from before the current dist/index.js build timestamp, making runtime freshness nondeterministic; live task/event observability has mixed 2.2.0 and 2.2.1 records today; latest codex_preflight_completed report was a diagnostic probe rather than proof of a real user-prompt hook; and the worktree is dirty with package.json/install.ps1 modifications that should be accounted for before claiming a clean release/runtime parity state.
